folio.label: 113v
folio.l: As fressú as is , the brigút Somers day
And so byfel , how that this goode man
Remembred hi~ , vp oon this Damyan
And seide , Seynt Marie , how may it be
That Damyan , entendetú nat to me
Is he ay syke , or how may it betyde
Hise squyers , whicú that stoden , æäe beside
Excused hi~ , by cause of his syknes
Whicú letted hi~ , to douö his besynes
Noon other cause , mygút make hi~ tarie
That me forthynketú , q¶ this Ianuarie
He is a gentil Squyer , be my trewthe
If that he deyed , it were harm and reuthe
He is as wys , discrete , and as secree
As ony man I knowe , of his degree
And therto manly , and eke seruysable
And for to be a man , thrifty and able
But after mete , as sone as euäe I may
I wyl my self , vysyte hi~ , and eke May
To don hi~ al the comfort , that I can
And for that word , hi~ blissed euäy man
That of his bounte , and his gentilnesse
He wolde so comfort , in sekenesse
His Squyer , for his gentil dede
Dame q¶ this Ianuarie , take good hede
At after mete , ©e witú ©oure wo~men alle
Whan ©e han ben in chaumbre , out of this halle
That alle ©e gon , to se this Damyan

note.notetxt: 679There is a cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: Dotú hi~ disport , he is a gentil man
And telletú hi~ , that I wyl hiö visite
Haue I no thyngö , but rested me a lyte
And spede ©ow fast , for I wyl a byde
Til that ©e slepe , fast be my syde
And with that word , he gan to hi~ calle
A Squyer , that was Marchal , of his halle
And told hi~ certeyne thynges , that hewoldehe woldehe/wolde
This fressú May , hatú streyt , hire weye holde
Witú alle hire wo~men , vn to Damyan
Doun by hise beddis syde , sytte she than
Comfortynge hi~ , as goodly as she may
This damyan , whan he , his tyme sey
In secrete wyse , his purs and eke his bylle
In whicú that he wryten had , his wylle
Hatú put in to hire hond , witú outen more
Saue that he sigúetú , wonder depe and sore
